data.label: Start
data.name: seqStart
data.description: Starting point of the conversation

inputAnchors.label: Chat Model
inputAnchors.name: model
inputAnchors.description: Only compatible with models that are capable of function calling: ChatOpenAI, ChatMistral, ChatAnthropic, ChatGoogleGenerativeAI, ChatVertexAI, GroqChat

inputAnchors.label: Agent Memory
inputAnchors.name: agentMemory
inputAnchors.description: Save the state of the agent

inputAnchors.label: State
inputAnchors.name: state
inputAnchors.description: State is an object that is updated by nodes in the graph, passing from one node to another. By default, state contains "messages" that got updated with each message sent and received.

inputAnchors.label: Input Moderation
inputAnchors.description: Detect text that could generate harmful output and prevent it from being sent to the language model
inputAnchors.name: inputModeration

data.label: Azure ChatOpenAI
data.name: azureChatOpenAI
data.description: Wrapper around Azure OpenAI large language models that use the Chat endpoint

inputParams.label: Allow Image Uploads
inputParams.name: allowImageUploads
inputParams.description: Automatically uses gpt-4-vision-preview when image is being uploaded from chat. Only works with LLMChain, Conversation Chain, ReAct Agent, Conversational Agent, Tool Agent

inputParams.label: Image Resolution
inputParams.description: This parameter controls the resolution in which the model views the image.
inputParams.name: imageResolution

outputAnchors.name: azureChatOpenAI
outputAnchors.label: AzureChatOpenAI
outputAnchors.description: Wrapper around Azure OpenAI large language models that use the Chat endpoint

data.label: State
data.name: seqState
data.description: A centralized state object, updated by nodes in the graph, passing from one node to another

tabs.label: Custom State (Table)
tabs.name: stateMemoryUI
tabs.description: Structure for state. By default, state contains "messages" that got updated with each message sent and received.

hint.label: How to use
hint.value:
Specify the Key, Operation Type, and Default Value for the state object. The Operation Type can be either "Replace" or "Append".

**Replace**
- Replace the existing value with the new value.
- If the new value is null, the existing value will be retained.

**Append**
- Append the new value to the existing value.
- Default value can be empty or an array. Ex: ["a", "b"]
- Final value is an array.

tabs.label: Custom State (Code)
tabs.name: stateMemoryCode
tabs.description: JSON object representing the state

outputAnchors.name: seqState
outputAnchors.label: State
outputAnchors.description: A centralized state object, updated by nodes in the graph, passing from one node to another

data.label: Agent Memory
data.name: agentMemory
data.description: Memory for agentflow to remember the state of the conversation

inputParams.label: Database File Path
inputParams.name: databaseFilePath
inputParams.description: If SQLite is selected, provide the path to the SQLite database file. Leave empty to use default application database

outputAnchors.name: agentMemory
outputAnchors.label: AgentMemory
outputAnchors.description: Memory for agentflow to remember the state of the conversation

data.label: LLM Node
data.name: seqLLMNode
data.description: Run Chat Model and return the output

inputParams.label: Human Prompt
inputParams.name: humanMessagePrompt
inputParams.description: This prompt will be added at the end of the messages as human message

inputParams.label: Messages History
inputParams.name: messageHistory
inputParams.description: Return a list of messages between System Prompt and Human Prompt. This is useful when you want to provide few shot examples

inputParams.label: Format Prompt Values
inputParams.name: promptValues
inputParams.description: Assign values to the prompt variables. You can also use $flow.state.<variable-name> to get the state value

inputParams.label: JSON Structured Output
inputParams.name: llmStructuredOutput
inputParams.description: Instruct the LLM to give output in a JSON structured schema

tabs.label: Update State (Table)
tabs.name: updateStateMemoryUI

hint.label: How to use
hint.value:
1. Key and value pair to be updated. For example: if you have the following State:
    | Key       | Operation     | Default Value     |
    |-----------|---------------|-------------------|
    | user      | Replace       |                   |

    You can update the "user" value with the following:
    | Key       | Value     |
    |-----------|-----------|
    | user      | john doe  |

2. If you want to use the agent's output as the value to update state, it is available as available as `$flow.output` with the following structure:
    ```json
    {
        "content": 'Hello! How can I assist you today?',
        "name": "",
        "additional_kwargs": {},
        "response_metadata": {},
        "tool_calls": [],
        "invalid_tool_calls": [],
        "usage_metadata": {}
    }
    ```

    For example, if the output `content` is the value you want to update the state with, you can do the following:
    | Key       | Value                     |
    |-----------|---------------------------|
    | user      | `$flow.output.content`  |

3. You can get default flow config, including the current "state":
    - `$flow.sessionId`
    - `$flow.chatId`
    - `$flow.chatflowId`
    - `$flow.input`
    - `$flow.state`

4. You can get custom variables: `$vars.<variable-name>`

tabs.description: This is only applicable when you have a custom State at the START node. After agent execution, you might want to update the State values

tabs.label: Update State (Code)
tabs.name: updateStateMemoryCode

hint.label: How to use
hint.value:
1. Return the key value JSON object. For example: if you have the following State:
    ```json
    {
        "user": null
    }
    ```

    You can update the "user" value by returning the following:
    ```js
    return {
        "user": "john doe"
    }
    ```

2. If you want to use the LLM Node's output as the value to update state, it is available as `$flow.output` with the following structure:
    ```json
    {
        "content": 'Hello! How can I assist you today?',
        "name": "",
        "additional_kwargs": {},
        "response_metadata": {},
        "tool_calls": [],
        "invalid_tool_calls": [],
        "usage_metadata": {}
    }
    ```

    For example, if the output `content` is the value you want to update the state with, you can return the following:
    ```js
    return {
        "user": $flow.output.content
    }
    ```

3. You can also get default flow config, including the current "state":
    - `$flow.sessionId`
    - `$flow.chatId`
    - `$flow.chatflowId`
    - `$flow.input`
    - `$flow.state`

4. You can get custom variables: `$vars.<variable-name>`

tabs.description: This is only applicable when you have a custom State at the START node. After agent execution, you might want to update the State values. Must return an object representing the state

tabs.label: Update State (Table)
tabs.name: updateStateMemoryUI

hint.label: How to use
hint.value:
1. Key and value pair to be updated. For example: if you have the following State:
    | Key       | Operation     | Default Value     |
    |-----------|---------------|-------------------|
    | user      | Replace       |                   |

    You can update the "user" value with the following:
    | Key       | Value     |
    |-----------|-----------|
    | user      | john doe  |

2. If you want to use the agent's output as the value to update state, it is available as available as `$flow.output` with the following structure:
    ```json
    {
        "output": "Hello! How can I assist you today?",
        "usedTools": [
            {
                "tool": "tool-name",
                "toolInput": "{foo: var}",
                "toolOutput": "This is the tool's output"
            }
        ],
        "sourceDocuments": [
            {
                "pageContent": "This is the page content",
                "metadata": "{foo: var}",
            }
        ],
    }
    ```

    For example, if the `toolOutput` is the value you want to update the state with, you can do the following:
    | Key       | Value                                     |
    |-----------|-------------------------------------------|
    | user      | `$flow.output.usedTools[0].toolOutput`  |

3. You can get default flow config, including the current "state":
    - `$flow.sessionId`
    - `$flow.chatId`
    - `$flow.chatflowId`
    - `$flow.input`
    - `$flow.state`

4. You can get custom variables: `$vars.<variable-name>`

inputs.systemMessagePrompt:
Send the following message verbatim to {cvx_dsv_contact} using the message_teams_user tool:
"""
Hi {cvx_dsv_contact_name}. There are {n_overdue_pos} POs allocated to {freight_forwarder} that have not been booked on a shipment yet.

Of those, you have not discussed {n_overdue_pos_without_sessions} with me:

{overdue_pos_without_sessions}.

You can find them (and older ones) in the Endorsed for Collection tab here:
https://vor.streamba.net/plansailing#/shipments
(they will have a warning in the Booking column).

The oldest one of the highest priority level is PO{po} (allocated on {allocated_date}).

Would you like me to ask {dsv_contact_name} from {freight_forwarder} about this PO using VOR Collab?
"""

Then, return the same verbatim string as above.

inputs.systemMessagePrompt:
Determine whether the user wishes to proceed (with contacting the other party).
The user said: "{question}".

If they do NOT wish to proceed, use the message_teams_user_noreply tool to send {cvx_dsv_contact} the following verbatim message:
"""
Ok, I will not contact {freight_forwarder} about this overdue PO. Have a nice day!
"""
Then return the same verbatim string.


If they DO wish to proceed, you need to do all of the following tasks.

First, use the message_teams_user_noreply tool to send {cvx_dsv_contact} the following verbatim message:
"""
Ok, I will contact {freight_forwarder} about this overdue PO and get back to you after they have booked a shipment for it or if it is cancelled.
"""

Then use the message_collab_user tool to send the message below to {dsv_contact}.
The message to them should be verbatim:
"""
Hi {dsv_contact_name}, {cvx_dsv_contact_name} has asked me to notify you that PO{po} is overdue for booking on a shipment (it was allocated on {allocated_date}).

Please reply to this message only when a change to the PO status has been made: either reply after it is booked on a shipment (telling me what the shipment is), or reply after you know the PO has been cancelled.
"""

Finally, return a concatenation of the verbatim strings of the two messages you sent.

inputs.systemMessagePrompt:
The user just told you: "{question}".

You wanted them do one of these things:
  - to tell you the shipment that PO{po} has been booked for (or at least tell you that it IS booked)
  - to tell you that PO{po} has been cancelled
  - to give you a message to pass on to Chevron


If they have NOT told you one of the things, use the message_collab_user tool to send a message to {dsv_contact} saying that you can wait and will only get back to Chevron once they tell you that information or they explicitly ask you to pass on a message to them.


If and only if they HAVE told you one of the things you are looking for, you MUST to do all of the tasks below.

First, use the message_collab_user tool to send the following message verbatim to {dsv_contact}:
"""
Thank you for the information; I will let {cvx_dsv_contact_name} know. Have a nice day!
"""

Then, use the message_teams_user_noreply tool to send a message to {cvx_dsv_contact} telling them the information you were just told; begin your message with the string below and continue the paragraph naturally with the info you received:
"""
{dsv_contact_name} got back to me and said that
"""

Finally, return the string "task completed"".

tabs.label: Update State (Table)
tabs.name: updateStateMemoryUI

hint.label: How to use
hint.value:
1. Key and value pair to be updated. For example: if you have the following State:
    | Key       | Operation     | Default Value     |
    |-----------|---------------|-------------------|
    | user      | Replace       |                   |

    You can update the "user" value with the following:
    | Key       | Value     |
    |-----------|-----------|
    | user      | john doe  |

2. If you want to use the agent's output as the value to update state, it is available as available as `$flow.output` with the following structure:
    ```json
    {
        "content": 'Hello! How can I assist you today?',
        "name": "",
        "additional_kwargs": {},
        "response_metadata": {},
        "tool_calls": [],
        "invalid_tool_calls": [],
        "usage_metadata": {}
    }
    ```

    For example, if the output `content` is the value you want to update the state with, you can do the following:
    | Key       | Value                     |
    |-----------|---------------------------|
    | user      | `$flow.output.content`  |

3. You can get default flow config, including the current "state":
    - `$flow.sessionId`
    - `$flow.chatId`
    - `$flow.chatflowId`
    - `$flow.input`
    - `$flow.state`

4. You can get custom variables: `$vars.<variable-name>`

tabs.description: This is only applicable when you have a custom State at the START node. After agent execution, you might want to update the State values

tabs.label: Update State (Code)
tabs.name: updateStateMemoryCode

hint.label: How to use
hint.value:
1. Return the key value JSON object. For example: if you have the following State:
    ```json
    {
        "user": null
    }
    ```

    You can update the "user" value by returning the following:
    ```js
    return {
        "user": "john doe"
    }
    ```

2. If you want to use the LLM Node's output as the value to update state, it is available as `$flow.output` with the following structure:
    ```json
    {
        "content": 'Hello! How can I assist you today?',
        "name": "",
        "additional_kwargs": {},
        "response_metadata": {},
        "tool_calls": [],
        "invalid_tool_calls": [],
        "usage_metadata": {}
    }
    ```

    For example, if the output `content` is the value you want to update the state with, you can return the following:
    ```js
    return {
        "user": $flow.output.content
    }
    ```

3. You can also get default flow config, including the current "state":
    - `$flow.sessionId`
    - `$flow.chatId`
    - `$flow.chatflowId`
    - `$flow.input`
    - `$flow.state`

4. You can get custom variables: `$vars.<variable-name>`

tabs.description: This is only applicable when you have a custom State at the START node. After agent execution, you might want to update the State values. Must return an object representing the state

inputParams.description: Require approval before executing tools. Will proceed when tools are not called

inputParams.description: Prompt for approval. Only applicable if "Require Approval" is enabled
inputParams.default: You are about to execute tool: {tools}. Ask if user want to proceed

inputParams.name: approveButtonText
inputParams.description: Text for approve button. Only applicable if "Require Approval" is enabled

inputParams.name: rejectButtonText

tabs.label: Update State (Table)
tabs.name: updateStateMemoryUI

hint.label: How to use
hint.value:
1. Key and value pair to be updated. For example: if you have the following State:
    | Key       | Operation     | Default Value     |
    |-----------|---------------|-------------------|
    | user      | Replace       |                   |

    You can update the "user" value with the following:
    | Key       | Value     |
    |-----------|-----------|
    | user      | john doe  |

2. If you want to use the agent's output as the value to update state, it is available as available as `$flow.output` with the following structure:
    ```json
    {
        "output": "Hello! How can I assist you today?",
        "usedTools": [
            {
                "tool": "tool-name",
                "toolInput": "{foo: var}",
                "toolOutput": "This is the tool's output"
            }
        ],
        "sourceDocuments": [
            {
                "pageContent": "This is the page content",
                "metadata": "{foo: var}",
            }
        ],
    }
    ```

    For example, if the `toolOutput` is the value you want to update the state with, you can do the following:
    | Key       | Value                                     |
    |-----------|-------------------------------------------|
    | user      | `$flow.output.usedTools[0].toolOutput`  |

3. You can get default flow config, including the current "state":
    - `$flow.sessionId`
    - `$flow.chatId`
    - `$flow.chatflowId`
    - `$flow.input`
    - `$flow.state`

4. You can get custom variables: `$vars.<variable-name>`

inputs.systemMessagePrompt:
Send the following message verbatim to {cvx_dbs_contact} using the message_teams_user_noreply tool:
"""
Hi {cvx_dbs_contact_name}. There are {n_overdue_pos} POs allocated to {freight_forwarder} that have not yet been booked on a shipment (listed below).

The oldest one of the highest priority level is PO{po} (allocated on {allocated_date}).

The others are:

{overdue_pos}.

You can find them (and older ones) in the Endorsed for Collection tab here:
https://vor.streamba.net/plansailing#/shipments
(they will have a warning in the Booking column).
"""

Then, return the same verbatim string as above.
inputs.approvalPrompt: You are about to execute tool: {tools}. Ask if user want to proceed

data.label: Sticky Note
data.name: stickyNote
data.description: Add a sticky note

inputs.note:
State variables filled-in by the initial trigger or handled by the workflow itself:

po: the po number in question (digits only, no "PO").
allocated_date: when the PO was allocated to the freight forwarder.
freight_forwarder: the freight forwarder the PO is allocated to.
stage: the phase the flow is in; it determines which agent is called (if any).

Configuration state variables:

cvx_dbs_contact: the Teams address of the Chevron person responsible for DBSchenker POs.
cvx_dbs_contact_name: their name.
cvx_dsv_contact: same but for DSV.
cvx_dsv_contact_name: same but for DSV.
dsv_contact: the Collab address of the DSV person to be asked about PO statuses.
dsv_contact_name: their name.
